introSection: Expedition Everest  is a steel roller coaster located at Disney's Animal Kingdom in Lake Buena Vista, Florida, USA. The ride takes place on a tall artificial mountain and was built by Vekoma. The Guinness Book of World Records listed Expedition Everest as the world's most expensive roller coaster at $100 million.

sections.title: History
sections.content: Expedition Everest officially opened on April 7, 2006.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Expedition Everest begins with a lift hill that travels up the snowy mountain. After some drops and curves, the ride travels up the lift hill and stops there momentarily. At this time, the transfer track below switches, and the trains then falls backwards through a new section of track. Next, the ride is held up at another transfer track, where it falls forwards into the rest of the ride.

sections.title: Station
sections.content: Expedition Everest's station has separate areas for loading and unloading.

fullText:
Expedition Everest  is a steel roller coaster located at Disney's Animal Kingdom in Lake Buena Vista, Florida, USA. The ride takes place on a tall artificial mountain and was built by Vekoma. The Guinness Book of World Records listed Expedition Everest as the world's most expensive roller coaster at $100 million.

Expedition Everest officially opened on April 7, 2006.

Expedition Everest begins with a lift hill that travels up the snowy mountain. After some drops and curves, the ride travels up the lift hill and stops there momentarily. At this time, the transfer track below switches, and the trains then falls backwards through a new section of track. Next, the ride is held up at another transfer track, where it falls forwards into the rest of the ride.

Expedition Everest's station has separate areas for loading and unloading.
